architecture_fit.note: Correct minimal mechanism (single scaled-dot-product head) but a softmax normalization bug means returned weights aren't a distribution, so the one-hot mass concentration the goal asks about is broken/inverted.

baseline_comparison.note: Only the framework's analytic uniform strawman is present; the attempt runs no explicit method-vs-strawman comparison and its Benchmark tab is non-functional.

faithfulness.note: Purely synthetic/analytic with no ablation or causal check, and the README never states this nor proposes one; the implementation doesn't faithfully realize a softmax anyway.

operating_range.note: Sweep spans margin 0->8 (>=2 orders), but the reported degradation is backwards (mass falls as margin rises) due to the normalization bug, so the range tells the wrong story.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Mechanism is fully hand-written (no training), but the broken softmax means it does not reproduce the target one-hot behavior.

visual_judgement.note: app.py has a Python syntax error (gr.Plotlabel=...) so it fails the import boot-check; also reads wrong filename bench.json, overwrites demo with an empty Blocks, and scales mass by 1.2, distorting data.

visualisation_rationale.note: README 'Why this visualisation' explains the three metrics vs margin, but the described chart distorts mass (x1.2) and doesn't actually run.

notes: The attempt picks the right minimal architecture (a single scaled-dot-product attention head), but a softmax normalization bug (denominator divides by exp(max).sum() instead of exp(logits-max).sum()) returns un-normalized weights, so the headline mass_on_correct decreases as the margin grows — the exact inverse of the one-hot concentration the goal measures. Only argmax accuracy survives (monotonic), reaching 1.0. The Gradio app also fails its import boot-check due to a syntax error and reads the wrong benchmark filename, so the visualization is non-functional; worst-of architecture/baseline/faithfulness lands this at fail.
